hero.title: Discover your Poet Personality
hero.subtitle: Your poems reveal more than style. They reveal how you feel, what you notice, and how you turn experience into language.
hero.body: The Poet Personality Analyzer studies your writing across imagery, emotional tone, themes, structure, rhythm, and voice to identify your poetic type. The more poems you submit, the more accurate your profile becomes.

proofStrip: Pattern-based analysis across your submitted poems

howItWorks.title: How the Analyzer Works
howItWorks.intro: Your Poet Personality is based on recurring patterns in your writing over time.

steps.title: Submit your poems
steps.body: Start with one poem. Add more over time to improve precision.

steps.title: Detect recurring signals
steps.body: The analyzer looks across emotional tone, imagery and symbolism, rhythm and sound, structure and lineation, vulnerability, recurring themes, narrative vs impressionistic tendencies, simplicity vs density, and directness vs ambiguity.

steps.title: Map your poetic center
steps.body: You receive a primary type, adjacent traits, and profile-strength level.

steps.title: Refine your profile
steps.body: One poem can reflect a mood. Several poems reveal a pattern.

groups.name: Visionaries
groups.description: Driven by intuition, meaning, depth, and discovery. They interpret the unseen and pursue truths that cannot be reached directly.

groups.name: Romantics
groups.description: Guided by beauty, intimacy, devotion, and emotional atmosphere. Their work centers on feeling deeply and expressing that depth with resonance.

groups.name: Truth-Tellers
groups.description: Defined by honesty, realism, and emotional courage. They face what hurts, what matters, and what deserves to be said.

groups.name: Makers
groups.description: Shaped by craft, form, delivery, and design. They build poems with structural intention and compositional control.

types.name: The Alchemist
types.shortBlurb: Turns pain, memory, and longing into transformation.
types.subtitle: The poet who makes beauty from what burns.
types.overview: The Alchemist turns feeling into transformation. Their poems often process pain, desire, memory, and change into language that feels intimate and redemptive.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: Readers who connect with The Alchemist might resonate with Rumi, Sylvia Plath, and Seamus Heaney.

body: The Alchemist writes from rupture, then finds language that turns wound into wisdom.
Your best work doesn’t deny darkness—it reworks it into something strangely luminous.

list: Symbolic language that evolves across a poem
Endings that feel earned, not decorative
body: You rarely leave a feeling untouched; you transform it.
At your strongest, readers feel accompanied through difficult emotional terrain.
Your shadow is over-saturation—too much intensity can blur the poem’s center.

writingStyle.intro: Your lines arc from rupture to renewal.
list: Metaphors of fire, ash, tide, skin, metal
body: Your structure often mirrors change itself: fracture, recognition, renewal.

loveRelationships.intro: In intimacy, you seek transformation with trust.
body: In intimacy, you seek bonds that can survive truth, loss, and reinvention.
When insecure, you may test devotion through intensity; when grounded, you offer profound emotional healing.

types.name: The Oracle
types.shortBlurb: Writes in symbols, signs, and hidden truths.
types.subtitle: The poet who senses what lies beneath the surface.
types.overview: The Oracle writes as if truth arrives in flashes. Their work is intuitive, symbolic, and suggestive rather than explicit.
types.idealTagline: You write what cannot be said directly.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: This type often feels close in spirit to Emily Dickinson, Rumi, and T. S. Eliot.

overview.intro: You speak through symbol before statement.
body: The Oracle doesn’t explain everything; it reveals in flashes.
Your poems feel like messages half-heard in a dream and remembered as truth.
You write the unsayable by circling it with charged images.
At your strongest, readers feel mysteriously understood.
Your shadow is obscurity—too much veil can weaken emotional access.

writingStyle.intro: Your poems imply first, then reveal.
body: You build resonance through omission, echo, and motif recurrence.

loveRelationships.intro: In relationships, you read subtext instinctively.
body: In love, you often sense shifts before they are spoken.
When regulated, you offer rare emotional attunement; when overwhelmed, you can retreat into silence or riddles.

types.name: The Architect
types.shortBlurb: Crafts poems with precision, structure, and control.
types.subtitle: The poet who builds with intention.
types.overview: The Architect values form. Their poems are deliberate, composed, and technically disciplined even when emotionally charged.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: People drawn to this type might also admire Elizabeth Bishop, Robert Frost, and T. S. Eliot.

body: The Architect values design: line, turn, proportion, restraint.
Even your most vulnerable poems feel intentionally engineered.

coreTraits.intro: Design is how your feeling thinks.
body: You believe form is not decoration—it is meaning.
At your best, your work is elegant, durable, and rereadable.
Your shadow is over-management—too much polish can mute spontaneity.
Your poems often reveal an internal blueprint readers can feel, even if they cannot name it.

loveRelationships.intro: In love, reliability and design matter to you.
body: In relationships, you show care through steadiness, consistency, and thoughtful action.
Under stress, you may default to control; in trust, you become deeply reliable and emotionally articulate.

types.name: The Seeker
types.shortBlurb: Writes to search, question, and understand.
types.subtitle: The poet always moving toward something deeper.
types.overview: The Seeker writes to discover. Their poems are driven by questions, longing, and the feeling that meaning is near but not fully grasped.
types.idealTagline: You write to find what matters.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: This type may resonate with Walt Whitman, John Keats, and Seamus Heaney.

overview.intro: You write to discover what is true.
body: The Seeker is animated by questions, thresholds, and becoming.
Your poems feel like journeys where insight arrives mid-walk, not at the starting line.
You are less interested in final answers than honest pursuit.
At your strongest, readers trust your searching mind.
Your shadow is perpetual incompletion—circling can replace arriving.

writingStyle.intro: Your syntax moves like thought in pursuit.
body: Your strongest poems pair curiosity with concrete emotional stakes.

loveRelationships.intro: In partnership, growth matters as much as closeness.
body: In connection, you value growth, reflection, and meaningful conversation.
When uncertain, you may keep seeking instead of settling; when centered, you bring depth, wonder, and evolution to partnership.

types.name: The Lover
types.shortBlurb: Led by intimacy, desire, and emotional connection.
types.subtitle: The poet who writes from the heart outward.
types.overview: The Lover centers intimacy. Their poems are tender, desirous, and emotionally immediate, often shaped by devotion, longing, and memory.
types.idealTagline: You write to touch and be touched.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: Readers who identify with this type might feel drawn to Pablo Neruda, Sappho, and John Keats.

overview.intro: You write from contact, ache, and closeness.
body: The Lover privileges intimacy: the body remembered, the voice missed, the hand almost reached.
Your poems translate attachment into immediate emotional presence.

coreTraits.intro: Attachment is your poetic center of gravity.
list: Themes of longing, devotion, and absence
body: You make readers feel the distance between people—and the desire to cross it.
At your best, your warmth is unforgettable and deeply human.
Your shadow is romantic over-identification—feeling can outrun proportion.

writingStyle.intro: Your diction is tactile and direct.
body: You create closeness quickly, often from the first line.

loveRelationships.intro: In love, you reach for closeness without armor.
body: You seek reciprocity, emotional presence, and sincere affection.
When hurt, you may idealize or cling; when secure, you become generous, grounding, and profoundly affectionate.

types.name: The Dreamer
types.shortBlurb: Lives in atmosphere, wonder, and emotional possibility.
types.subtitle: The poet who drifts between reality and imagination.
types.overview: The Dreamer writes in atmosphere. Their poems often feel luminous, wistful, and emotionally suspended between memory and imagination.
types.idealTagline: You write what longing looks like in the air.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: This type often shares a similar energy with John Keats, Bashō, and Emily Dickinson.

overview.intro: You write in atmosphere before argument.
body: The Dreamer lives in the soft threshold between memory and imagination.
Your poems feel suspended—like light in dust, like a room after rain.
You compose emotional weather as much as narrative content.
At your strongest, readers are carried into a living interior landscape.
Your shadow is diffusion—too much haze can thin emotional stakes.

writingStyle.intro: Your poems drift with deliberate control.
body: Your best work balances lyric mist with one clear human anchor.

loveRelationships.intro: In relationships, emotional weather matters deeply.
body: You are drawn to emotional possibility, tenderness, and shared reverie.
When ungrounded, projection can replace presence; when grounded, you offer romance with real emotional care.

types.name: The Muse
types.shortBlurb: Drawn to beauty, elegance, and emotional allure.
types.subtitle: The poet who makes language feel magnetic.
types.overview: The Muse prioritizes beauty and tonal elegance. Their writing is often lyrical, polished, and emotionally attractive from the first line.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: People close to this type may admire Sappho, Pablo Neruda, and Mary Oliver.

body: The Muse makes language seductive without sacrificing coherence.
Your poems often arrive polished, magnetic, and quotable.
You understand that style can be substance when handled with precision.

strengthsShadows.intro: You make beauty memorable, not fragile.
body: At your best, your lines linger in memory and conversation.
Your shadow is surface over core—ornament can overtake emotional risk.

writingStyle.intro: Your line work is polished and musical.
body: You write with a keen ear for line beauty and tonal consistency.

loveRelationships.intro: In intimacy, admiration and presence go together.
body: In intimacy, you notice details and cultivate moments that feel artful and meaningful.
When insecure, performance can replace vulnerability; when open, you become deeply affectionate and emotionally elegant.

types.name: The Devotee
types.shortBlurb: Writes with sincerity, reverence, and care.
types.subtitle: The poet who honors what matters.
types.overview: The Devotee writes with reverence. Their poems return to what deserves care: memory, love, faith, grief, ritual, and place.
types.idealTagline: You write with devotion, not distance.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: This type might resonate with Mary Oliver, Rumi, and Walt Whitman.

overview.intro: You write with reverence for what matters.
body: The Devotee returns to love, memory, place, ritual, and sacred attention.
Your poems feel like offerings: careful, sincere, and emotionally clean.
You treat subjects with care rather than spectacle.
At your strongest, your poems restore trust in tenderness.
Your shadow is over-earnestness—irony and play may disappear.

writingStyle.intro: Your pacing is steady, ceremonial, and warm.
list: Grounded imagery of home, body, earth
body: You create continuity through recurring motifs and moral warmth.

loveRelationships.intro: In love, loyalty is a daily practice.
body: You value commitment, consistency, and shared meaning.
When strained, you may over-give; when balanced, you create relationships that feel safe, honored, and enduring.

types.name: The Confessor
types.shortBlurb: Speaks with raw honesty and emotional exposure.
types.subtitle: The poet who says what others avoid.
types.overview: The Confessor writes from exposure rather than distance. Their poems are direct, vulnerable, and emotionally fearless.
types.idealTagline: You write the truth before it is comfortable.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: Readers drawn here might resonate with Sylvia Plath, Anne Sexton, and Maya Angelou.

overview.intro: You write what others avoid saying aloud.
body: The Confessor is driven by raw truth, personal risk, and emotional disclosure.
Your work often reads like necessity rather than performance.

coreTraits.intro: Truth arrives unmasked in your lines.
body: You use poetry as an act of honest exposure.
At your best, readers feel less alone in their own complexity.
Your shadow is emotional flooding—confession can outrun shaping.

writingStyle.intro: Your delivery is intimate, stripped, and immediate.
body: Your strongest pieces pair vulnerability with deliberate craft.

loveRelationships.intro: In connection, truth is your first language.
body: In relationships, you want realness over politeness.
When dysregulated, intensity can overwhelm; when grounded, you offer extraordinary honesty and emotional loyalty.

types.name: The Witness
types.shortBlurb: Observes the world with quiet depth and realism.
types.subtitle: The poet who notices what others miss.
types.overview: The Witness writes through attention. Their poems preserve ordinary detail, social texture, and understated human truth.
types.idealTagline: You make the ordinary impossible to ignore.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: This type often feels close to Elizabeth Bishop, Langston Hughes, and Seamus Heaney.

overview.intro: You write by paying close attention.
body: The Witness preserves what is easily overlooked: gestures, streets, weather, ordinary grief.
Your poems build meaning through precise observation.
You trust evidence on the page more than abstraction.
At your strongest, your restraint creates deep emotional impact.
Your shadow is distance—recording can eclipse revelation.

writingStyle.intro: Your scenes do the emotional speaking.
body: You reveal feeling by showing the world clearly.

loveRelationships.intro: In partnership, you show love by noticing.
body: You show care by remembering details and honoring reality as it is.
When guarded, you may understate need; when open, you become a deeply dependable and emotionally perceptive partner.

types.name: The Rebel
types.subtitle: The poet who writes against what feels dead.
types.overview: The Rebel resists the safe version. Their poems push against falsehood, inherited rules, and emotional or political silence.
types.idealTagline: You would rather disrupt than pretend.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: People drawn to this type might admire Allen Ginsberg, Audre Lorde, and Adrienne Rich.

overview.intro: You write against numbness and pretense.
body: The Rebel is oppositional in service of truth, life, and freedom.
Your poems push form, language, and expectation to create real contact.
You reject dead language and inherited scripts.

strengthsShadows.intro: You jolt language back to life.
body: At your best, your work wakes readers up.
Your shadow is reaction without integration—defiance can flatten complexity.

writingStyle.intro: Your form prefers voltage and fracture.
body: Your strongest poems channel anger into shape, not noise.

loveRelationships.intro: In intimacy, freedom and honesty are non-negotiable.
body: You need authenticity, freedom, and mutual courage in connection.
When threatened, you may escalate or withdraw; when secure, you become fiercely protective and electrifyingly honest.

types.name: The Mourner
types.shortBlurb: Holds grief, memory, and tenderness with dignity.
types.subtitle: The poet who gives sorrow a voice.
types.overview: The Mourner writes with elegiac gravity. Their poems hold endings, absence, and remembrance with tenderness and control.
types.idealTagline: You know how to hold what is gone.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: This type may resonate with readers who love Sylvia Plath, Anne Sexton, and Elizabeth Bishop.

body: The Mourner gives language to absence without sentimentality.
Your poems honor grief as a living relationship, not a solved event.

coreTraits.intro: Elegy is your way of staying close.
body: You create space where loss can be held, named, and shared.

strengthsShadows.intro: You hold grief with unusual grace.
body: At your best, your work provides solace and recognition.
Your shadow is tonal narrowing—sorrow can eclipse other colors.

writingStyle.intro: Your cadence is patient and elegiac.
body: You often write from aftershocks, where detail becomes devotion.

loveRelationships.intro: In love, remembrance is part of devotion.
body: You carry people deeply, even across distance and time.
When burdened, you may linger in ache; when supported, you offer extraordinary tenderness, loyalty, and emotional depth.

types.name: The Storyteller
types.shortBlurb: Builds poems through scene, character, and unfolding.
types.subtitle: The poet who moves through moments.
types.overview: The Storyteller thinks in scenes. Their poems unfold through sequence, character, and emotional progression.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: This type might resonate with Maya Angelou, Langston Hughes, and Seamus Heaney.

overview.intro: You think in scenes and movement.
body: The Storyteller builds poems that unfold rather than merely declare.
Your strongest work carries readers through lived moments with momentum.
You trust story as a vessel for feeling and meaning.
At your best, your poems are vivid, humane, and memorable.
Your shadow is over-explanation—plot can crowd lyric lift.
You often land impact through ending reversals or final reframing.

loveRelationships.intro: In relationships, shared stories become glue.
body: You bond by telling, listening, and co-creating meaningful memories.
Under stress, you may narrate instead of feel; in balance, you bring warmth, play, and deep relational continuity.

types.name: The Minimalist
types.shortBlurb: Uses restraint, silence, and precision for impact.
types.subtitle: The poet who trusts what is left unsaid.
types.overview: The Minimalist writes with compression and intent. Their poems distill feeling into clean lines, controlled space, and exact language.
types.idealTagline: You know how little a poem needs to stay.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: Readers close to this type might appreciate Bashō, Emily Dickinson, and Robert Frost.

body: The Minimalist compresses emotion into clean, durable language.
Your poems prove that silence and spacing can carry as much force as statement.
You remove until only the necessary remains.

strengthsShadows.intro: You make silence do real work.
body: At your best, each line carries disproportionate weight.
Your shadow is emotional coolness—spareness can feel withholding.

writingStyle.intro: Your edits cut to the essential.
body: Your strongest poems leave room for reader participation without losing center.

loveRelationships.intro: In connection, you favor clarity over spectacle.
body: You value honesty, simplicity, and emotional signal over dramatic display.
When defended, you can seem distant; when open, you offer steadiness, trust, and deeply intentional care.

types.name: The Performer
types.shortBlurb: Writes with rhythm, voice, and spoken energy.
types.subtitle: The poet who knows how a line lands out loud.
types.overview: The Performer writes for breath and impact. Their poems carry cadence, momentum, and oral presence.
types.idealTagline: You write to be heard and remembered.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: This type often shares energy with Maya Angelou, Langston Hughes, and Allen Ginsberg.

overview.intro: You write for breath, room, and impact.
body: The Performer hears the body in language: cadence, pause, strike, release.
Your poems are built to land aloud and stay remembered.

coreTraits.intro: Cadence is where your authority lives.
body: You understand performance as craft, not just charisma.

strengthsShadows.intro: You command a room with language.
body: At your strongest, your work electrifies and connects instantly.
Your shadow is overprojection—punch can replace depth.

writingStyle.intro: Your rhythm is built for breath and memory.
body: You often shape poems around how they move through a listener, not only a page.

loveRelationships.intro: In love, expression is generous and visible.
body: You bring enthusiasm, language, and visible devotion to relationships.
When unsteady, intensity can dominate; when grounded, you bring joy, courage, and unforgettable affirmation.

types.name: The Weaver
types.shortBlurb: Braids image, meaning, and emotion into layered work.
types.subtitle: The poet who connects everything beneath the surface.
types.overview: The Weaver creates layered coherence. Their poems braid symbol, thought, memory, and emotional tension into textured wholes.
types.idealTagline: You make complexity feel woven, not scattered.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: People who identify with this type may be drawn to Adrienne Rich, T. S. Eliot, and Emily Dickinson.

body: The Weaver interlaces memory, symbol, thought, and feeling across layers.
Your work rewards rereading because meaning accumulates with each pass.

coreTraits.intro: Integration is your superpower on the page.
body: You are skilled at holding contradiction without collapsing it.
At your best, your poems feel rich without feeling random.
Your shadow is density overload—too many threads can diffuse focus.

writingStyle.intro: Your motifs interlock across the whole piece.
body: You compose in networks rather than single-line statements.

loveRelationships.intro: In intimacy, you bond through nuance and pattern.
body: You connect through nuance, memory, and meaningful synthesis.
When stressed, you may over-process; when regulated, you offer profound understanding and emotionally intelligent companionship.

resultsPage.title: Your Poet Personality Profile

sections.name: secondaryTraits
sections.title: Adjacent Traits
sections.description: Neighboring energies that regularly appear in your writing.

sections.name: confidence
sections.title: Profile Strength
sections.description: Confidence increases with more submitted poems.

sections.name: signalSummary
sections.title: Why this result
sections.description: A concise summary of recurring signals behind your match.

sections.name: refineCta
sections.title: Refine your profile
sections.description: Submit another poem to strengthen precision over time.

resultsPage.footnote: Poet Personalities are pattern maps, not fixed labels. Your profile can evolve as your writing evolves.

states.empty: Start with one poem. Your profile deepens over time.
states.errorShort: This text may be too short for reliable analysis.
states.errorGeneric: We couldn't analyze that poem. Please try again.

positioningLines: A personality system that reads your poems to reveal the kind of poet you are.
Submit your poems. Discover your poetic type. Refine it over time.
One poem captures a moment. A body of poems reveals a voice.
